interaction_phrase: advocates like blockchain educator Andreas Antonopoulos and the geeky Russian-Canadian Vitalik Buterin
occurrence_sentence: There's no doubt of the disruptive capabilities of the blockchain. It's changed our lives in more ways than one. With its advocates like blockchain educator Andreas Antonopoulos and the geeky Russian-Canadian Vitalik Buterin backing up the system's efficacy on a daily basis from interviews and keynote speeches, the technological legacy left from Satoshi Nakamoto's groundbreaking whitepaper, which birthed Bitcoin, seems to those against it a barrage against the status quo, a self-made battering ram prepared to break the ancient wall's of the establishment, centralized economy and world order.
With its advocates like blockchain educator Andreas Antonopoulos and the geeky Russian-Canadian Vitalik Buterin backing up the system's efficacy on a daily basis from interviews and keynote speeches,
Antonopoulos and Buterin think blockchain's lock-and-loaded, so pumped up on technological Rambo juice that it's the only sheriff in town.

This has only been compounded by Google's recent claim to achieving 'Quantum Supremacy'. In a recent article, published in The International Business Times, Dr. Xinxin Fan, an expert on security and privacy for the IoT, Cloud computing, blockchain and cryptography at IoTeX, discussed how the blockchain community needs to be educated on the developments made in QC.
